zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady, Joan Whitney於1291年出生在英國越可爾斯凱爾頓時，她的父親William Whitney28歲，母親Female Whitney21歲。她於1317年與Ralph Andrews II在英國越可爾曼諾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭越可爾曼諾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady, Joan Whitney于1291年出生在英国越可爾斯凱爾頓时，她的父亲William Whitney28岁，母亲Female Whitney21岁。她于1317年与Ralph Andrews II在英国越可爾曼諾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世在英国英格兰越可爾曼諾。

nameMeanings.name: Whitney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Whitney in Herefordshire, the etymology of which is uncertain. The second element is Old English ēg ‘island, piece of higher ground in a low-lying area’; the first appears to be hwītan, which is either the genitive singular of an Old English byname Hwīta (meaning ‘white’), or the weak dative case (originally used after a preposition and article) of the adjective hwīt ‘white’. The name may also derive from Whitney (now Whitney Wood) in Stevenage (Hertfordshire), probably named from Old English hwītan ‘white’ + (ge)hæge ‘enclosure’.
History: John Whitney came from London, England, to Watertown, MA, in 1635, and had numerous prominent descendents.

variants.name: Witney
meanings: English: habitational name from Witney in Oxfordshire, which for centuries was a centre of the wool trade, famous in particular for the manufacture of blankets. The placename derives from the Old English personal name Witta (genitive Wittan) + Old English ēg, īeg ‘island’. There may have been some confusion with Whitney .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady, Joan Whitney was born in 1291, in Skelton, Yorkshire, England, her father, William Whitney, was 28 and her mother, Female Whitney, was 21. She married Ralph Andrews II in 1317, in Manor, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died in Manor, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady, Joan Whitney est née en 1291, à Skelton, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, William Whitney, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Female Whitney, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Ralph Andrews II en 1317, à Manor, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée à Manor, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady, Joan Whitney 1291 in Skelton, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Whitney 28 und ihre Mutter Female Whitney 21. Sie hat 1317 in Manor, Yorkshire, England Ralph Andrews II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb in Manor, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady, Joan Whitney è nata il nel 1291 nel Skelton, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, William Whitney, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Female Whitney, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Ralph Andrews II nel 1317, nel Manor, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel Manor, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady, Joan Whitney(이)가 1291년 영국 요르크실르 스켈턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Whitney(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Female Whitney(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1317년 영국 요르크실르 먼어에서 Ralph Andrews II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 먼어에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady, Joan Whitney родилась в 1291 г. в местности скелтон, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Whitney было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Female Whitney, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ralph Andrews II в 1317 г. в местности манор, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в местности манор, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady, Joan Whitney nació en 1291, en Skelton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Whitney, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Female Whitney, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Ralph Andrews II en 1317, en Manor, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en Manor, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
